chunk_summary: Stepwise management of pediatric hypertriglyceridemia focuses on lifestyle changes, lipid panel monitoring, and specialist referral for severe cases to reduce metabolic risk and prevent complications like pancreatitis.
learning_objectives: Understand fasting triglyceride thresholds for pediatric hypertriglyceridemia management.
Learn stepwise interventions for triglyceride levels ranging from mild to severe elevations.
Recognize when to refer pediatric patients to lipid specialists for advanced care.
Identify secondary drivers and complications of severe hypertriglyceridemia, including pancreatitis.
key_facts: Mild-to-moderate hypertriglyceridemia: Ages 10–19 triglycerides 130–199 mg/dL; ages 0–9 triglycerides 100–199 mg/dL.
Triglycerides 200–499 mg/dL: Target both triglycerides and non-HDL-C (<145 mg/dL).
Triglycerides ≥500 mg/dL: Prioritize pancreatitis risk management and specialist involvement.
Triglycerides ≥1,000 mg/dL or abdominal pain with vomiting: Urgent evaluation for pancreatitis and hospital care required.
Omega-3 supplementation (fish oil) may be considered if non-HDL-C remains ≥145 mg/dL after lifestyle interventions.
reference_quote: The NHLBI algorithm recommends stepping from CHILD-1 to the triglyceride-focused CHILD-2-TG pattern and pairing this with weight management.
document_key_topics: Lifestyle interventions for dyslipidemia in children
Specialist referral for severe triglyceride elevations
Pancreatitis risk and urgent care in extreme cases
